title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
why_it_matters: Historical baseline for agent evaluation before the 2025 shift to process diagnostics and live SWE benchmarks.
notes: Keep as historical context, not as a sufficient 2026 deployment proxy.

title: Introducing SWE-bench Verified
why_it_matters: Human-validated 500-instance subset that became the most cited reliable coding-agent regression set.
notes: Updated 2025-02-24; pair with the official SWE-bench site for leaderboard context.

title: Training Software Engineering Agents and Verifiers with SWE-Gym
why_it_matters: Bridges training and evaluation by turning software engineering into an interactive environment for agents and verifiers.
notes: Useful pre-2025 context for mid/post-train discussions.

title: Introducing the Model Context Protocol
why_it_matters: Marks the protocolization turn: model-tool-data connectivity becomes an ecosystem layer rather than ad-hoc glue.
notes: Use as the historical launch point; pair with the living spec for current details.

title: Preparedness Framework / system cards
why_it_matters: Frames agent autonomy and software-engineering capability as deployment-governed risk, not just leaderboard progress.
notes: Policy-oriented evidence; pair with technical evals.

title: Why Do Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail?
why_it_matters: Provides a reusable failure taxonomy and cross-framework trajectory data, making MAS errors inspectable rather than anecdotal.
notes: Repository and data are available; use as the diagnostic anchor.

title: SWE-bench Goes Live!
why_it_matters: Adds continuous, contamination-resistant software engineering evaluation closer to real deployment conditions.
notes: Best read as a complement to Verified, not a replacement.

title: M-MAD: Multidimensional Multi-Agent Debate for Advanced Machine Translation Evaluation
why_it_matters: Shows how multi-agent debate can be operationalized as an evaluation system with released code/data.
notes: ACL metadata provides month-level publication date (2025/7).

title: Multiple LLM Agents Debate for Equitable Cultural Alignment
why_it_matters: Represents the coordination-plus-fairness line that matters for non-SWE, socially grounded MAS tasks.
notes: Keep for topical coverage even though open assets are limited.

title: DEBATE: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Evaluating Opinion Dynamics in Role-Playing LLM Agents
why_it_matters: Important scope-completion benchmark for social-interaction realism, not just production-direct workflows.
notes: Retain even though it is less directly deployment-oriented than SWE benchmarks.

title: Coordinated LLM multi-agent systems for collaborative question-answer generation
why_it_matters: Represents application-layer collaborative QA rather than only coding and enterprise ops, broadening the repo back to the report's stated scope.
notes: Crossref shows 2025-11 issue date; public code availability should be verified independently.

title: Sonar Claims Top Spot on SWE-bench leaderboard
why_it_matters: Shows benchmark results being translated into product positioning and enterprise messaging by 2026.
notes: Treat as an industry signal rather than a neutral technical evaluation.

title: OpenAI Platform Documentation (Agents / Responses / tracing)
why_it_matters: Living reference for handoffs, tracing, and tool-mediated runtimes; useful as a 2026Q1 engineering snapshot.
notes: Snapshot date is the repository capture date for a living doc.

title: Model Context Protocol Specification
why_it_matters: Living spec for the protocol layer that the 2025-2026 ecosystem increasingly builds around.
notes: Snapshot date is the repository capture date for a living spec.

title: LangGraph Overview / Repository
why_it_matters: Canonical graph-style orchestration reference for explicit state transitions, durable execution, and HITL.
notes: Use alongside the repo for API/version details.

title: Microsoft AutoGen (repo and release line)
why_it_matters: Major multi-agent runtime line with continued investment in teams, memory backends, and MCP-related integration.
notes: Release notes are especially relevant for RedisMemory and nested-team evolution.

title: LlamaIndex Multi-Agent Workflows
why_it_matters: Documents layered multi-agent patterns for knowledge-centric systems rather than only coding agents.
notes: Living docs; snapshot dated at repo capture time.

title: CrewAI
why_it_matters: Popular high-level role/team abstraction and useful contrast point for role-oriented MAS design.
notes: Keep as a representative high-level framework, not just as a production recommendation.
